hypothesis_en: Mr President, Mr President-in-Office of the Council, Mr Commissioner, ladies and gentlemen, this is a serious hour in which we must work even harder to break the infernal spiral of violence in the Middle East. We strongly condemn the murder of the leader of Hamas and the other Palestinians by direct order of the Israeli government, and we also strongly condemn the murders and terrorist acts against the civilian population of I think we should appeal to the Palestinians not to respond with an infernal increase in terrorist acts and more bloodshed. This murder has already had very serious consequences, including the suspension of the Arab League summit. So we must put an end to this policy of continuing to build the wall within the occupied territories, ordering extrajudicial executions and, in addition, comply with the withdrawal of Gaza, to which the current Israeli government has clearly committed itself. The violation of human rights, the failure of the Israeli government to comply with the latest UN resolutions and, above all, Ariel Sharon's insistence on resuming executions outside the international legal framework, left us no choice but to reiterate the position adopted by this House in the resolution of 10 April 2002 and to ask the Commission and the Council to strictly implement Article 2 of our Association Agreement. We do not want to get to this, but we believe that a solemn and severe warning must be made, and in this sense I want to make three requests. The Presidency invites the Member States of the European Union, those who are members of the United Nations Security Council, to present immediately a proposal to establish an international interposition force at the borders of 1967 with the mandate to protect both sides. I think that we need to frame this whole process within the proposals that are being made to be able to solve the Middle East issue. We must propose peace rather than start new wars and increase the cycle of violence, and above all in a situation where the stability of those regimes that could contribute to supporting a reform process is being broken. The Arab League and all the countries of the region can continue along the lines of the declaration on the common future for the Middle East recently put forward by Minister Fischer of Germany, and in which those things could be included in which the European Union, which is the first partner, as the Commissioner of the region has rightly said, we could contribute many elements in terms of peace and security treaties. I conclude, Mr President, by saying that the European Parliament has contributed its sandstone with the creation of the Parliamentary Assembly of Athens and that we have a duty not only political but also moral to continue the European Union's efforts in the Barcelona process to overcome such a crisis. I would like to ask the President-in-Office of the Council whether he is aware of the fact that the Council of Ministers of the European Communities has adopted a resolution on the situation in the former Yugoslavia.
reference_en: thank you (1.34) very much President (1.15) President Office of Council Commissioner Honourable Members (1.51) (2.12) we have to work together o= once more and more than ever to break the vicious circle that affects the Middle East (1.01) Mr Yassin may not have been among our best friends but his assassination is a clear and flagrant breach of international law (1.46) we could have captured him and tried him don't forget that we do have an International Court of of Justice an International Criminal Court of Justice th= th= that isn't recognised by everybody unfortunately including Israel so the assassination of the Hamas leader and other Palestinians has taken place on the orders of a government the Israeli government and we condemn that and we condemn all terrorist acts against civilian population (2.14) and I would just (1.12) call on the Palestinians not to respond by increasing terrorist acts on their part (2.38) this assassination has had very serious impact including the suspension of the summit of the Arab League and here (1.02) I would refer to Shimon Peres in the Knesset when he said that there would be even more serious consequences as a result of that I think that we should bring an end to this policy where there is a building of a wall in the occupied territories with extrajudicial assassinations (2.66) and the the the stop to the withdrawal from Gaza which which has been promised by the Israelis I would say that the continuing violations of human rights the failure to to by the Israelis to meet the terms of the recent (1.06) UN (2.47) resolutions and extrajudicial killings ordered by Sharon means that we have to reiterate the position adopted on the tenth of April two thousand and two in this House calling on the Council and Commission to apply properly Article two of the Association Agreement we don't want to go there we don't want to go down that path but we really feel that something needs to be done as a= a clear signal needs to be given we need to to call on three things first of all we need to call on civil society (1.07) in Israel all the political parties in Israel all while defending their own security that they require that their government act in in in accordance with international law secondly we should call on the Presidency to call on the Member States of the European Union who are in the UN Security Council immediately to table a proposal for setting up an international force (1.86) for respecting the nineteen (1.26) sixty-seven borders with the idea of both sides being protected thirdly the Commission should be called on to continue with its EU assistance so that the Palestinian Authority can be strengthened but also be reformed because we do not have a strong counterpart in the Palestinian Authority at the moment and that's what we need (1.95) I really think that all of this process needs to be situated within the (1.11) proposals that are being tabled for the Middle East solution as a whole obviously there there's a profound problem there it's better to propose peace rather than proposing further wars and escalating the the vicious circle of violence (1.2) especially because stability in those regimes which could continue to support the reform process is being undermined (1.52) so we need to return to strengthening a process in which the European Union is involved the Eur= the the United States obviously has a great responsibility as well the Arab League and all the Arab countries everybody needs to follow the line of the declaration on the future of the Middle East that Minister Fischer r= referred to in Germany recently so that the European Union= we are the number one partner as the Commissioner quite rightly said in the region we need to gather together many of these elements to promote peace security arms control respect for human rights and democracy and economic cooperation in the region in conclusion President let me say that the European Parliament has brought its interests into the region with the the creation of this Parliamentary Assembly in Athens and we have a duty not just a political one but a moral one to continue to fight to strengthen the European Union in the line of the Barcelona process so that we can overcome this profound and entrenched crisis which calls on our political economic and moral responsibility thank you very much
source_es: gracias señor Presidente señor Presidente del Consejo señor Comisario Señorías esta es una hora grave en la que tenemos que trabajar aún más para romper la infernal espiral de violencia en Oriente Medio aunque el jeque Ahmed Yassin no figuraba entre nuestros mejores amigos su asesinato es una clara y flagrante violación del derecho internacional hubíamos podido hubieran podido detenerle y juzgarle y recuerdo además que hemos creado un Tribunal Internacional de Justicia que debe de tener un papel aunque no es apoyado por algunos Estados entre ellos Israel condenamos rotundamente el asesinato del líder de Hamás y de los demás palestinos por orden directa de un gobierno del Gobierno de Israel condenamos también rotundamente los asesinatos y actos terroristas contra la población civil de ambas partes del conflicto ~y_ creo que debemos hacer un llamamiento a los palestinos para que no respondan con un infernal aumento de los actos terroristas y con más derrame de sangre (1.05) este asesinato ya ha tenido consecuencias muy graves entre ellas la suspensión de la Cumbre de la Liga Árabe y en este sentido yo quiero compartir el juicio severo de Shimon Peres en la Knesset cuando dijo que este hecho traería todavía consecuencias más graves (1.26) por lo tanto hay que ca= acabar con esta política de seguir construyendo el muro dentro de los territorios ocupados ordenando ejecuciones extrajudiciales y además cumplir con la retirada de Gaza de a la cual se ha comprometido claramente el actual Gobierno israelí y quiero decir en nombre de mi Grupo que las repetidas violaciones de derechos humanos el incumplimiento de las últimas resoluciones de las Naciones Unidas por parte del Gobierno israelí y sobre todo la insistencia de Ariel Sharon de reanudar las ejecuciones fuera del marco legal internacional no nos dejarán otra opción que reiterar la posición adoptada por esta Cámara en la resolución del diez de abril de dos mil dos y pedirle a la Comisión y al Consejo la aplicación estricta del artículo segundo de nuestro Acuerdo de asociación no queremos llegar a esto pero creemos que hay que hacer una advertencia solemne y severa y en este sentido quiero hacer tres peticiones la primera es invitar a la sociedad civil a esa vibrante sociedad israelí y a todos sus partidos políticos que defendiendo el derecho a su propia seguridad que le exijan a su Gobierno que actúe de acuerdo con el derecho internacional en segundo lugar pedirle a la Presidencia que invite a los Estados miembros de la Unión Europea aquellos que forman parte del Consejo de Seguridad de las Naciones Unidas a que presenten inmediatamente una propuesta para establecer una fuerza internacional de interposición en las fronteras de mil novecientos sesenta y siete con el mandato de proteger a ambas partes tercero también que la Comisión continúe con su ayuda con la ayuda de la Unión Europea para que la Autoridad Nacional Palestina se refuerce y se reforme porque hace falta un interlocutor fuerte si defendemos que tiene que haber un Estado palestino (1.16) ~y_ creo que hay que enmarcar todo este proceso dentro de las propuestas que se están haciendo para poder resolver el tema de Oriente Medio es evidente que para resolver un conflicto tan enconado y tan profundo es mejor proponer la paz que iniciar nuevas guerras y aumentar el ciclo de la violencia y sobre todo en una situación en la que se está rompiendo la estabilidad de aquellos regímenes que podrían contribuir a apoyar un proceso de reforma y por lo tanto creemos que debemos de volver a iniciar y reforzar un proceso en el que la Unión Europea los Estados Unidos que tienen también una grave responsabilidad la Liga Árabe y todos los países de la región puedan continuar en la línea de la declaración sobre el futuro común para Oriente Medio que planteó recientemente el ministro Fischer de Alemania y en la que podrían entrar aquellas cosas en las que la Unión Europea que es el primer socio como ha dicho aceptadamente el Comisario de la región nosotros podríamos aportar muchos elementos en lo que respecta a tratados de paz seguridad control de armas desarme respeto a derechos humanos y democracia y cooperación económica y concluyo señor Presidente diciendo que el Parlamento Europeo ha aportado su granito de arena con la creación ya por fin de la Asamblea Parlamentaria de Atenas y nosotros tenemos un deber no sólo político sino también moral de continuar con el esfuerzo de la Unión Europea en la línea del proceso de Barcelona para conseguir superar una crisis tan enconada como profunda que comporta y que nos interpela en nuestra responsabilidad política económica y moral muchas gracias señor Presidente (1.11)
